response: Implemented in May 2018, the EU's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) mandates strict data handling standards for any organization processing EU residents' information, granting individuals rights like data access, erasure, and explicit consent. Non-compliance carries severe penalties of up to 4% of global annual revenue or €20 million, with regulators already levying massive fines against tech giants like Meta (€1.2 billion), Amazon (€746 million), and Google (over €200 million). Despite criticisms over heavy compliance burdens and inconsistent enforcement, the GDPR has successfully established data privacy as a fundamental right and set a global benchmark that has inspired similar legislation worldwide.
